title: Student — Enrolment
content:
Overview


The Enrolment page is where students request to join teacher-led subjects at their school. It shows available courses created by teachers and tracks the status of submitted requests.



Available Courses


This section lists all subjects created by teachers at the student's school. Key features:


  • Term filter: Dropdown to filter by term (Fall, Spring, Winter, Summer, September Term, January Term, May Term, or All Terms)
  • Year selector: Plus/minus buttons to change the academic year
  • Search: Filter available courses by subject name
  • Collapsible: Click the section header to expand or collapse the list

Each available course shows the subject name and a Request Enrolment button. Clicking it submits a request to the teacher who created that subject.



My Enrolment Requests


This section tracks all enrolment requests the student has submitted. Each request shows:


  • Subject name: The course requested
  • Status: Pending (awaiting teacher review), Enrolled (approved by teacher), or Rejected (declined by teacher)
  • Date: When the request was submitted

Request statuses explained:


  • Pending: The teacher has not yet reviewed the request. No action is needed — the student should wait for the teacher to respond
  • Enrolled: The teacher approved the request. The subject now appears in the student's My Courses page
  • Rejected: The teacher declined the request. The student can submit a new request or contact the teacher for clarification

Rejected requests can be deleted by the student to keep their enrolment history clean.



How Teachers Process Requests


Teachers see enrolment requests in the Pending section of their Enrolment page in the Teacher Portal. They can approve or reject each request. Once approved, the student appears in the teacher's gradebook and receives assignments for that subject.



Key Talking Points for Agents


  • "Use the term and year filters to find subjects for the right semester."
  • "Pending means the teacher hasn't reviewed your request yet — give them some time."
  • "If you're enrolled, the subject will appear in My Courses."
  • "You can delete rejected requests if you want to clean up your list."
  • "If you don't see any available courses, your teachers may not have created subjects for that term yet."
